---
title: "satRdays: an introduction"
author: "Patrick Durkee"
date: 2019-01-01 #"`r noquote(format(Sys.Date(), '%Y-%m-%d'))`"
categories: ["satRdays"]
tags: ["satRdays"]
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE)

noquote(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d"))

```

I'm planning to make a (semi-)weekly habit of learning a new thing in R (e.g., a function, an analysis, a feature) and writing a breif overview/tutorial of it. This will be the first of the series.

The main motivation for starting this series is to contribute to the community that helped me---and continues to help me---learn R. Great resources for learning R are already scattered across the internet, but I figure one more voice couldn't hurt. 

For this one, I'll just list and describe some of my favorite resources for learning R.
